moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit-scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove weakness.
Exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Buy XLF to diversify into a strong new sector, enhancing portfolio quality.
Increase cash to 20% for capital preservation and future opportunities.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting a potential ~1.5%/month. It significantly reduces risk by cutting leverage and weak holdings, but may limit upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and rebalance broad index exposure.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit-scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove weakness.
Exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to reduce overall portfolio risk.
Buy XOM and AMD to diversify into strong sectors with high-scoring setups.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month with a focus on risk-first growth. It reduces concentration and leverage while adding quality setups, aiming for steady appreciation with moderate drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and free up capital for high-conviction trades.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit-scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove weakness.
Exit all current leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) as they are below their stops.
Buy XOM, AMD, and GUSH, concentrating in the highest-scoring scanner setups.
Utilize GUSH for leveraged exposure to a strong sector, accepting higher risk.
Maintain a minimal 5% cash buffer to maximize deployed capital.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It involves higher concentration and strategic use of leveraged ETFs, which significantly increases both upside and potential drawdown. Leveraged ETFs are subject to daily decay and rebalancing, demanding strict stop discipline.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure.
Sell all holdings flagged by the exit-scanner (QQQI, QQQ, SMH) to remove weakness.
Exit all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) and trim CRWD to reduce high-beta exposure.
Buy XLF and RTX to add stable, high-quality names to the portfolio.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation and liquidity.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and high-beta holdings, focusing on stability. Distributions are variable and NAV can still decline in market downturns.
